comment_text.sn35.242:1.9: This refers to the pārājika offences—sexual intercourse, murder, stealing, and lying about spiritual attainments—which entail immediate and permanent expulsion from the Saṅgha.

root_text.sn35.242:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kimilāyaṁ viharati gaṅgāya nadiyā tīre.
root_text.sn35.242:1.2: Addasā kho bhagavā mahantaṁ dārukkhandhaṁ gaṅgāya nadiyā sotena vuyhamānaṁ.
root_text.sn35.242:1.4: “passatha no tumhe, bhikkhave, amuṁ mahantaṁ dārukkhandhaṁ gaṅgāya nadiyā sotena vuyhamānan”ti?
root_text.sn35.242:1.6: evaṁ vutte, āyasmā kimilo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.242:1.9: Idha, kimila, bhikkhu aññataraṁ saṅkiliṭṭhaṁ āpattiṁ āpanno hoti yathārūpāya āpattiyā na vuṭṭhānaṁ paññāyati.

translation_text.sn35.242:0.3: The Simile of the Tree Trunk (2nd)
translation_text.sn35.242:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Kimibilā on the bank of the Ganges river.
translation_text.sn35.242:1.2: Seeing a large tree trunk being carried along by the current,
translation_text.sn35.242:1.4: “Mendicants, do you see that large tree trunk being carried along by the current of the Ganges river?”
translation_text.sn35.242:1.6: When this was said, Venerable Kimbila said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn35.242:1.7: “But sir, what’s the near shore and what’s the far shore? What’s sinking in the middle? What’s getting stranded on high ground? What’s getting taken by humans or non-humans? What’s getting caught up in a whirlpool? And what’s rotting away?” …
translation_text.sn35.242:1.9: It’s when a mendicant has committed the kind of corrupt offense for which no rehabilitation is possible.

variant_text.sn35.242:1.9: āpattiyā na vuṭṭhānaṁ → āpattiyā vuṭṭhānaṁ (bj, pts1ed)
